content:
First, label the elements of M in increasing order by  
a₁=1, a₂=2, a₃=4, a₄=5, … so that in general  
 if j=2k (=even) then aⱼ=3k−1,  
 if j=2k+1 (=odd)  then aⱼ=3k+1.  

We look for a block of length 2n, say aₘ,…,a_{m+2n−1}, whose sum is 300.  One checks two cases (m even or odd) and finds in each case the sum reduces to an equation of the form  
 3n·(…)=300 ⇒ n·(…)=100,  
so that n must divide 100 and make an auxiliary parameter p integral and ≥0 (or ≥1).  Carrying this out yields exactly five solutions:  

n=1,2,4,5,10.  

Hence the possible values of n are  
\boxed{1,2,4,5,10}.
